finding: A verified queue artifact and deterministic owned-item selector still leave an unaudited gap unless the selected item, exact registered owner, and immutable source commit are bound into one startup evidence record before implementation begins.

observed: The wrapper maps the five registered team names to exact queue owner ids.
The wrapper invokes the existing verified owned-item selector rather than reimplementing queue discovery.
An accepted startup record requires a selected item, exact owner match, and an immutable verifier commit.
The record contains a deterministic SHA-256 digest over owner, selected item, and verified source commit.
Source inspection found and corrected an initial commit-field mismatch before evidence publication.
inferred: A runtime that requires accepted team-start evidence can make the actual autonomous work-selection decision auditable and fail closed when selection provenance is absent.
proposed: Wire this contract into each team automation runtime before implementation steps and retain each accepted or refused startup packet as append-only evidence.
superseded: Treating verification of the queue artifact alone as proof of which work item an autonomous team actually selected.
Allowing a human-readable team label to drift independently from the canonical queue owner id.
unresolved: The full Node test suite was not executed in a fresh checkout because the available container could not resolve github.com.
No automation runtime is yet proven to invoke this contract before implementation.
The startup evidence packet is digest-bound but not cryptographically signed.

verification.method: GitHub connector commit plus post-commit source inspection
verification.runtime_blocker: Fresh clone failed because github.com DNS resolution was unavailable in the execution container.

privacy_boundary: Public repository operational metadata, abstract queue items, registered team names, synthetic fixtures, and commit identities only. Private chats, health and pet-health material, credentials, personal identifiers, and unreviewed uploads are excluded.
falsification_route: The contract fails if an unregistered team, foreign-owner item, selector refusal, missing item, or missing immutable commit produces accepted=true; it also fails if semantically identical selections produce different digests solely because object key order differs.
